# duplocloud_aws_s3_table (Resource)

`duplocloud_aws_s3_table` manages an S3 table bucket in Duplo.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "duplocloud_tenant" "myapp" {
  account_name = "myapp"
  plan_id      = "default"
}


resource "duplocloud_aws_s3_table" "table" {
  tenant_id = duplocloud_tenant.myapp.tenant_id
  name      = "table"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the S3 table bucket.
- `tenant_id` (String) The GUID of the tenant that the S3 table bucket will be created in.

### Optional

- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `arn` (String) The ARN of the S3 table bucket.
- `created_at` (String) The timestamp when the S3 table bucket was created.
- `fullname` (String) The full name of the S3 table bucket as assigned by Duplo.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `owner_account_id` (String) The AWS account ID of the S3 table bucket owner.

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String)
- `delete` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Example: Importing an existing S3 table for an AWS resource
#  - *TENANT_ID* is the tenant GUID
#  - *S3_TABLE_NAME* The non prefix name of the s3 table.
#
terraform import duplocloud_aws_s3_table.table *TENANT_ID*/*S3_TABLE_NAME*
```
